answer:
Binance APIs support three types of authentication keys: Ed25519, RSA, and HMAC [1][2]. Ed25519 is the recommended method for its superior performance and security [1].

Authentication for signed requests involves using your private key to sign request parameters, which Binance verifies using your public key [1]. For WebSocket connections, you can authenticate via the session.logon method to avoid providing an API key and signature in every subsequent request [3][4]. While HMAC is supported, it is currently deprecated in favor of asymmetric cryptography methods like Ed25519 and RSA [1].

results.title: API Key Types | Binance Open Platform
